abstract: The proliferation of multisensor technology and the explosion of big data have exposed the inherent limitations of traditional flat-view matrix models, signaling a critical need for more versatile analytical frameworks. We demonstrate that higher-order tensors—or multiway arrays—facilitate a fundamental paradigm shift toward models that are essentially polynomial in nature. Crucially, unlike conventional matrix methods, the uniqueness of these tensor models is guaranteed under remarkably mild and natural conditions. Leveraging the mathematical rigor of multilinear algebra, tensor decompositions offer unparalleled flexibility in applying constraints that align with intrinsic data properties. This approach enables the extraction of more complex latent structures, far surpassing the capabilities of standard matrix-based techniques.
